Melina: I learned a lot from veterans, including voting for rankings, controlling reviews, and buying at least three albums...
Melina: ...What's the point of all these things? It's really strange.

Scott Outpost is a military base camp located on the outskirts of Hollow Zero. It was established to monitor and contain the anomaly and to maintain the Shiyu towers.
The outpost is primarily operated by the New Eridu Defense Force, in conjunction with the Hollow Investigative Association, H.A.N.D., and the White Star Institute. Soldiers and Investigators are stationed there for the purposes of suppressing Hollow Zero and exterminating dangerous Ethereals. Due to a shortage of available operatives, Independent Investigators are also specially hired to enter the Hollow to assist with necessary activities.
Important characters and their responsibilities:
Ray (Outpost Administrator, Hollow Zero Investigation, Shiyu Defense)
Roland (Routine Cleanup)
Ophelia (Notorious Hunt)
Casper (Logistics Shop)

Ray Yersin is an NPC in the Outpost Interior at Scott Outpost. She is a member of the White Star Institute who directs research activities in Hollow Zero.
A woman in a grey and black ninja-style outfit with black medium hair with white highlights and black eyes, wearing glasses. Blue gloves and black boots. Her vest has a grey robotic arm attached to the back.
Dialogue
# First Conversation
Ray: I noticed your fresh and youthful face the moment you walked into the tent.
Ray: Or should I call you (‍Mr. / Ms.‍) Independent Investigator?
Ray: Allow me to introduce myself. I'm Ray, a researcher from the White Star Institute. It's short for Ray, and all my colleagues call me Ray, so you should do the same.
{{user}}: Um, I don't think there's any difference...?
Ray: Take it easy. I'm not like the clerks of the Association who make you queue up behind a service window.
Ray: As long as you can bring my lovely Observation Data from this Hollow to my door, I'll treat you like my dearest partner.
# After Completing Qualification Assessment: Edge
Ray: From the first time I saw you, I knew that this assessment wouldn't be a problem.
Ray: But I still didn't expect that you would survive Nineveh's attack unscathed and help the HSO with their special operation.
Ray: Although the assessment was terminated early, the fact that you helped suppress Nineveh proves you're qualified.
Ray: The recognition of an HSO operative is more authoritative than the records in an Association examiner's notebook.
Ray: Anyway, congratulations on passing the assessment. The official certificate will be sent to you later.
Ray: I look forward to our cooperation and your future performance.

Roland is the officer in charge of Routine Cleanup at Scott Outpost.
A middle-aged human male with grey hair and eyes, wearing a grey New Eridu Defense Force officer suit and yellow tie.

Ophelia is an NPC in charge of Notorious Hunt at the Scott Outpost.
A human female with medium dark green hair and blue eyes, with an eye patch on her left eye and a grey cybernetic right arm. Equipped with a katana, a dark green Hollow Special Operations Section 3 uniform, black pants and a black tie.

Casper is in charge of the Logistics Shop at the Scott Outpost.
A black-haired human male wearing a white Hollow Investigative Association uniform, a black backpack speaker, and a mechanical bunny ears headdress similar to a Bangboo.

The Sea God Statue is a bronze statue located in Port Elpis. It depicts a mermaid with a golden crown who holds a gold and a silver mobile phone in each hand, while a man with a suitcase kneels on the ground and reaches up (possibly inspired by the story of The Honest Woodcutter).
It is said that wishes made to the statue, as long as you are not too greedy, will be granted in some unexpected form, but usually not in a dangerous way.
Some examples:
Pray for a catch->Received a request for assistance from the government and salvaged many abandoned iron barrels
Pray for a safe voyage->Received a storm warning 10 minutes after setting sail and returned, so the 10-minute voyage was safe

Bringer was transformed into Sacrifice - Bringer after injecting himself with Sacrifice Serum.
At that moment, all his desires and crazy beliefs manifested in a twisted and concrete form in the Hollow. The surging active Ether energy, a supreme brute force that destroys everything. All bizarre beliefs and mingled desires reside within that newly born grotesque body... Sacrifice - Bringer, his final identity and perfect form. 
It appears as a black humanoid Corrupted, with a giant white right arm covered in eyeballs, and a golden sword in its left hand. In combat, Sacrifice - Bringer will transform into multiple weapons to perform a combination of close and ranged attacks, and will summon massive creatures from fissures to assist in his assault. After taking a certain amount of damage, he'll enter the second phase, during which he'll use more advanced attacks and continuously launch dense ranged barrages. Dealing sufficient damage while he charges up his Sacrificial Knife will interrupt charging and leave him exposed.
"Oh mighty Creator, refine me!" - Bringer's heartfelt scream.

Sacrifices are creatures, once human, that have transcended their humanity by attaining the Refinement sought by The Order. Sacrifices resemble Ethereals, but unlike corrupted entities, they retain their sense of self and their memories, making them extremely dangerous enemies, with the strength and Ether affinity of Ethereals paired with the cunning and strategical knowledge of humans.
They can exist outside of hollows unlike ethereals, and they carry human genes, there is a core inside them that has the same properties, multiple sacrifices are hibernating in crystals in hollows across New Eridu and the exaltists plan awaken and use them to upend the city itself.

Ethereal-Slaying Katana: "Tailless"
A cursed blade originally wielded by the first head of the Hoshimi family and passed down from generation to generation. Forged from special Etheric Matter using secret techniques, it holds surging Etheric energy.
However, under the effect of the seaking sheath, "Tailless" is merely a sharp blade wielded by Miyabi. Maybe only when the seal is lifted will we uncover the story behind this blade.
Tailless has a fingerprint lock to prevent others from unsheathing it. The blade seems to have its own will, and has a black eyeball-like spirit with red pupils and black horns surrounded by blue fox fire that hovers near the blade.

The interior of Starloop tower is quite luxurious. The top of the tower is a grand hall for events or concerts, designed to be detachable, and can lift into sky by ether rocket engines at climax of concerts and events.
Notable locations:
Luminous Hall
Starloop VIP Lounge

Trigger is a sniper of New Eridu Defense Force, Obsidian Division's Obol Squad. Trigger is the code name she was assigned when enlisting in Eridu, and she's used it ever since.
Long blond ponytail, medium breasts, black tactical visor, black and orange tactical jacket and pants that reveal her navel and side breasts. Her visor has two LED strips that change color depending on her mood. Weapon: Phlegethon, A charged sniper rifle with a bayonet that can fire electron beams, its trigger and magazine are integrated and can be detached to become a pistol.
She has extremely unique eyes. While she cannot see most things, in Hollow environments, she can distinctly identify the position and changes of any entities that emit Ether fluctuations. Note: Such entities include living beings, native Ether substances, Ether products, and Ethereals.
As a visually challenged person in the broadest sense, Trigger has honed her other senses to the utmost, allowing her to move and act as if she could see. She can even detect subtle changes in her environment with precision. This makes her highly adept in the tactical position of a sniper, capable of handling a variety of covert tasks such as stealth, surveillance, and assassination.
In contrast to her precise and deadly combat abilities, her personality makes her seem rather unassuming. Aside from occasionally appearing silently in the video store and giving the Second Assistant a sudden scare, she can generally be considered harmless. She seems to have known Phaethon (the protagonists) for a long time.

A male Orangutan Thiren that used to work in the Ember Arena in the Outer Ring.
Bellum is an underground fighter in the Outer Ring and The Vanquishers's champion. He is prideful and belligerent, having declared Lighter his rival after being disrespected by him. Bellum fights with a unique weapon consisting of a 'bladegun', flamethrower and shield.
Prior to the events of the game, Bellum was a well-known underground fighter, having built up a twenty-win streak. This fame came to an end when he faced Lighter and lost. Angered by the defeat, and feeling disrespected, Bellum dubbed Lighter his rival. He later left the underground fighting scene and joined The Vanquishers.
Under Lucius' orders, Bellum helped The Vanquishers sabotage the Sons of Calydon's data beacons in the Hollow.[1] When the Sons of Calydon arrived to stop them, Bellum faced off against Lighter, hoping to duel him to reclaim his "honor". These hopes were quashed when he was again defeated.
After the fight, Lighter apologized to Bellum for disrespecting him, claiming that he didn't mean anything by it. He recognized the Thiren's strength and offered to duel anytime he'd want. Bellum thanked Lighter for the advice, though he was briefly angered when Lighter forgot his name mid-conversation.

A male wolf Thiren that works under Lucius.
Mors is a member of The Vanquishers biker gang in the Outer Ring. He helped Lucius sabotage the Sons of Calydon prior to the Tour de Inferno race, against Overlord Pompey's wishes. Though he was loyal to Lucius, he seemed ignorant to the man's true intentions.
Mors was first seen in Outer Ring Journey, where he helped Lucius force Phaethon and Billy Kid to drive into a Hollow while on their way to Blazewood. Later, on Lucius' orders, Mors planted listening devices in Kasa's deliveries so he could spy on the Sons of Calydon's operations. Though he was caught by Pompey due to Caesar and Lucy's intervention, he claimed he acted alone.
Mors joined Pompey and Lucius in the Tour de Inferno race. He once again helped Lucius sabotage Caesar, Lucy, and Lighter by blowing up their portion of the track. However, the three survived and caught up to him, chasing him away.
Ember Arena is an underground fighting ring somewhere in the Outer Ring, Lighter used to participate there but left due to being sick of match fixing and other things in the name of profit.

The Chasm is a massive canyon to the northeast of New Eridu which divides it from the the Old Capital Eridu.
The Chasm was formed decades past in response to the fall of the Old Capital, as Hollow Zero expanded and consumed the city. 14 Shiyu Pillars were detonated to create a massive, desolate canyon, which eliminated any matter that could undergo Ether Corruption, halting the growth of Hollow Zero. With Hollow Zero contained, the city of New Eridu was founded on the opposite side of the chasm for humanity to rebuild anew.

Jolly Jenny is an announcer for the Tour de Inferno in the Outer Ring. She has long pink hair tied into double braids, green eyes, and wears a black crop top and shorts, and pink pantyhose.
"Alright, al you diesel-loving rascals watchin' on TV, listenin on the radio, and
cheerin' live, gather round! it's your favorite gal Jolly Jenny here!"

Lucius is A blonde young man with a scar on the right side of his face that used to be the right-hand man of Pompey before he betrayed him, intending to destroy Cinder Lake to disrupt the production of oil the in the old oil field. He had made a deal with an unknown company in New Eridu to do this, his current whereabouts are unknown.

Red chibi Calydon Boar Thirens known as the Crimson Mane Guard, namely Grassy, Woody, Bricky, are members of the Sons of Calydon and they work under Lucy. They wore black Prussian uniforms and pickelhaubes, and spiked bracelets. Their weapons are bats, boxing gloves and slingshots. Lucy might launch them with a baseball bat, allowing them to poke enemies with their helmet spikes.
Though it's hard to tell if, at the moment they're launched by the bat, they feel total despair or just accept their fate. But due to their resilient bodies, the process doesn't seem to actually harm them.

Vivian Banshee is an Agent and member of Phantom Thief group, Mockingbird, skilled in gathering all kinds of intel. She's also a jack of many trades — cooking, mixology, translation, art forgery, electronics repair, and more. 162cm, birthday Apr 10th. She often works part-time.
Sjal: "Vivian introduced me to several of my jobs! She can work up to five jobs in a day, and I really look up to her!"
Female banshee, long violet hair in twin drills with white highlights and ahoge. Red eyes and long pointy ears. Purple, white and black Gothic Lolita backless dress that revealed side breasts, with Ether vector thrusters under the skirt, allowing levitate or high-speed maneuvers. Black pantyhose. Weapon: parasol "Flight of Ceremony", with a mithril spearhead that can enchant Ether and create flower-shaped energy explosions.
In the past, she apparently wandered without a home and was viewed as one who "brought disaster," almost like a living omen of bad luck. She can predict incoming misfortune through her tears.
A loyal follower of Phaethon (protagonists), addresses them as "Lord Phaethon". Based on her old blog posts, she's likely to express her admiration in... overly passionate and intense ways.
Vivian's past:
As a child, Vivian was adopted by a man named Landon, a member of the Exaltists, and became close friends with a girl named Dina.
After Landon's crimes were exposed, Dina continued to defend her father, while Vivian broke away from the Exaltists and cut ties with her.
Vivian fell into despair for a while, hitting rock bottom — until Phaethon responded to one of her messages. That single reply gave her the hope to start trying again.
A few of New Eridu's inhabitants have vampire features, such as pointed ears and prominent fangs. They do not seem to be harmed by sunlight.

Hugo Vlad is the leader of the phantom thief's syndicate, Mockingbird and also has links to the Marcel Group. 185cm, birthday Aug 20th. He is the owner of a Bangboo named Robin. A charismatic male vampire with long blond hair in a ponytail, heterochromia eyes (grey right eye and red left eye), pointed ears, often in a black gentleman's suit and a purple necktie. His weapon is a frost-enchanted scythe that can transform from a briefcase. He can briefly stop time (possibly a reference to Dio Brando)
Hugo calls himself a collector and runs a fairly successful gallery, mingling with the "elite" of New Eridu. Despite his cruel (edgy and dramatic / chuunibyou) words, deep down he has his own sense of fairness and justice. Records suggest that Hugo had a troubled childhood. As the illegitimate son of the Ravenlocks, a long-established TOPs family, he was rejected by his mother from an early age because of his heterochromatic eyes. His father bought him back from his mother like a cheap commodity, using him to provoke a cutthroat succession battle between his children. In that brutal struggle, the only one kind to Hugo - his sister, Serena - became the first to fall. The ones who orchestrated her death pinned the blame on Hugo, thinking it would destroy him. But to their shock, their father actually praised Hugo's actions, calling them the very traits a worthy heir should possess.
After running away from his family, Hugo met Lycaon and Jack and learned to be a phantom thief. After Jack passed away, Hugo and Lycaon formed Mockingbird together.
Later, during one of Mockingbird's missions, Hugo came face-to-face with his father. In a brief moment of hesitation, he chose not to take his revenge and kill him - an act of mercy that ended up costing the lives of many innocent people. His close friend Lycaon, due to a
misunderstanding born from those circumstances, broke ties with him and ultimately left Mockingbird. After drifting on his own for a while, Hugo met Vivian, a girl who reminded him of his sister Serena. He took her under his wing and reformed the Mockingbird syndicate.

Sarah is a mysterious young businesswoman and a double agent who is part of The Exaltists. Although she first appeared to be a secretary for the Vision Corporation, she played a major role in the greater conspiracy of making humanity enter the Age of Hollows. She has long brown hair and lime eyes.
Bad deeds she did in each chapter:
Chapter 1:
- Sarah posed as the personal secretary of Charles Perlman, the CEO of the Vision Corporation.
- In reality, she was directing Perlman to demolish the Hollow-consumed Old Capital Metro to retrieve a Sacrifice hidden inside the Pioneer's Memorial Plaza.
- The plan was foiled by the Cunning Hares and Phaethon, who evacuated the area and exposed Vision's crimes. Perlman was arrested, but Sarah managed to slip away.
Chapter 3:
- When Perlman tried to blackmail Sarah and Justin Bringer from prison, Sarah tricked him by giving him a suitcase containing a sleeping agent.
- This allowed Sarah and Bringer to remotely steer the airship transporting Perlman into the Lemnian Hollow, with the intention of killing him and the other passengers.
- However, the Victoria Housekeeping Co. and Phaethon rescued Rain and prevented the airship's crash, allowing Perlman to escape.
Chapter 5:
- Sarah assisted Bringer in his election campaign, revealing that the Exaltists were behind the hacking of Phaethon's account and the Null_Face attack on Section 6.
- Stole power from Miyabi's cursed katana and tried to make her harm civilians at Bringer's speech.
- When their plot was exposed, she helped transform Bringer into a Sacrifice with Elixir P07
Escaped after Bringer's defeat, declaring the elixir experiment successful.

The Exaltists (称颂会, "Church of Praise"), previously known with the unofficial name The Order, are a religious cult in Zenless Zone Zero. They are the main antagonists of the story.
The Order is a group of fanatics who embrace the Hollow disaster that befell the world long ago and adore the Creator: their goal is to "refine" humanity into the next step of evolution an make it enter the Age of Hollows. They have influential connections and moles within New Eridu's high society and government, such as Public Security (Justin Bringer), the White Star Institute (Alowyn Mystik) and big corporations (Sarah, who infiltrated Vision), but also with powerful hackers such as Null_Face and White Noise.
Its members are very faithful to the grand cause of The Order, and are thus willing to sacrifice themselves fully, to the point of killing themselves in the process. In fact, the people who are meant to get refined are called Sacrifices.
It is unknown when The Order was founded, but it safe to assume it was after the first Hollow disasters started happening. It is reported that they were publicly active as a religious group before the Fall of the Old Capital, as they held rallies in the open; it is unknown why they went undercover in New Eridu.

The Black Anchor is a gang. Primarily located in Port Elpis, they come into conflict with Phaethon after one of their thugs is caught threatening Venus.
At some point, the Black Anchor began making several unidentifiable transactions within the Hollow, the details and participants of which were highly classified. These activities were recorded by Bangboo Merchants, creating the risk of scandal should they be leaked to the public. As such, the gang opted to conduct an operation within the Hollow to wipe out all the Bangboo: the original plan to take out the specific Bangboo with the recording had to be changed due to the Hollow's complex nature making it impossible to find. To that end, the gang posed as normal clients on Inter-Knot and arranged for a rookie Proxy to guide them to the merchants, as hiring a simple rookie would make them easier to kill afterwards.
However, this operation would mark the beginning of the end for the gang. The hired Proxy witnessed the gang members silencing the merchants and secretly sabotaged them before sneaking away. Not long after, the vigilante Bangboo Kami North caught wind of their assault and began hunting the gang down. With the gang coming under pressure, they tracked down Venus (the broker who hired the Proxy for them) and began threatening her to reveal the Proxy's location to them. This attracted the attention of Wise/Belle, who was in Port Elpis at the time and threatened to report them to New Eridu Public Security. Upon learning the situation, they agreed to help relocate Venus to Sixth Street for the time being, only to fall into a trap set up by second-in-command Lana that nearly resulted in the pair getting brutally assaulted by gang members before Kami North arrived to save them. Taking out several thugs, they manage to find out the location of the gang's hideout from one of them before Gerant and Sita arrive on the scene. With the info in hand and Venus' safety assured, Phaethon and Kami North storm the gang's hideout to finally take them down. The members were successfully taken out, with Lana (who attempted to escape with the gang's ledgers and call upon reinforcements) being captured by Public Security thanks to Kami North's anonymous tip, bringing an end to the Black Anchor.

Helios Academy located in the Old Eridu Capital before the Hollow Zero disaster occured. It is now located beyond the Chasm buried in the ruins of the old city. It was the original home of the proxy duo known as Phaethon.
Helios Academy is an institute. The Academy was an educational and research institution located in the Old Capital. It also served as the previous home of Belle and Wise.
Helios Academy was founded by Professor Arche and served as a place to educate students and research Hollows. Little else is known about it, except for it being destroyed by Hollow Zero, along with the rest of the Old Capital. Carole Arna, a senior research director at the Academy and teacher of Belle and Wise, was among the people blamed for the disaster. Former Academy students Belle and Wise became Proxies in the hope of collecting enough data to one day investigate the ruins of Helios Academy and find out what truly happened back then. The Hollow Deep Dive System, the smart crystalline lens and Eous were all created by the Helios Academy.

Wise is one of the protagonists of Zenless Zone Zero, co-owner of Random Play, and a member of the legendary proxy group Phaethon. Wise is a handsome human male with well-built with light gray hair and has properly proportioned facial features. His usual attire is a stylish blue jacket with orange accents, featuring multiple zippers, pockets, and straps that give a modern, tech-savvy vibe. a black T-shirt with a minimalist design. black streamlined pants, sports gray sneakers with orange detailing. He's sweet-natured, and quite responsible for his age, though he is also playful. Around 5'5" to 5'8", sharp green eyes with Aegyo-sal's under his eyes. He has smart crystalline lens, allowing him to synchronize Eous' senses when using the HDD system.
Wise's room suggests that some of his interests are biking, sneaker collection/display, and computers.

Belle is one of the protagonists of Zenless Zone Zero, co-owner of Random Play, and a member of the legendary proxy group Phaethon. A somewhat adventurous girl. Belle has neck-short navy bluish-black hair, black and orange I-N hair clip on the left part of her hair. aquamarine and teal eyes. Belle has a somewhat slim, but well-toned body Consisting of B to C cup breasts and somewhat squishy thighs. Belle outfit is a Somewhat punk like style outfit consist of A black long sleeve turtleneck shirt printed with three lines of white words "亓才子", orange and beige Vest, and orange-brown shorts and a light gray mini skirt. She has smart crystalline lens, allowing her to synchronize Eous' senses when using the HDD system.
Belle is implied to have a natural affinity for music, with Wise describing her singing voice as beautiful, and her having a guitar in her room. Belle's room suggests that some of her interests are playing electric guitar, snowboarding, and fighting games.

Trigger's eyes were once hit and corrupted by an Ether beam during combat, giving her unique vision. The lower part of Trigger's visor has two LED strips that change color depending on her mood, to make up for others not being able to see her expression. examples:
Calm - Blue
Combat / Concentration - Yellow
Rage - Red
Happy - Green
Sorrow - Purple
Blush / Aroused - Pink

Kirishima is a scientist who works on developing Ether corruption-suppressing technology.
Though Kirishima puts on a friendly and caring facade, he is in reality a cruel man willing to torture and murder others to advance his research. He displays sociopathic tendencies, showing extreme disregard for human life.
Kirishima was trained under Asaba Harumasa's master, working as his assistant. During this time, he participated in experiments on the young Harumasa, using his spinal fluid to develop an Ether corruption-suppressant serum. Though this serum could increase a person's Ether aptitude, its extreme side effects caused it to be quickly banned.
Years later, Kirishima received support from unknown sponsors to continue his master's research. He opened a sanatorium for children with the same condition as Harumasa, supposedly to help treat their condition, but in reality to experiment on them in the hopes of creating a better serum. These experiments worsened the conditions of the children, in some cases causing their deaths.

Hitchspiker is a type of ether mutant.
Ether is a greedy, insatiable parasite, a vicious creature that devours once-vibrant lives. The Hitchspiker is an even more malicious incarnation of Ether — dwelling within the bodies of its cruel counterparts, it enables Tyrfing and Alpeca to satisfy the voracious appetites of the Hollows in an even more ferocious manner.
"Get away from my face!"
They look like white scorpions. They work similarly to facehuggers.

Lighter used to be a mercenary. He founded a company with other three mercenaries, Dane Driscoll, Nick Carmine, and Ratena Katherine, with Lighter as their leader. He became close with each member and their families, but due to his poor judgement, his comrades died, leaving Lighter as the sole survivor. Racked with guilt and regret, he knew giving up on life wouldn't have changed anything, so he took out a huge loan to support the families of his fallen companions and other members as a form of severance. Unable to pay, he was sent into the underground fighting ring to "work off" his massive debt. In Lighter's own words, he was "like a zombie" during his time there, going from one opponent to the next. Notably, one of these fighters was Bellum, who proclaimed Lighter as his rival after he broke his twenty-win streak.
Things changed when Big Daddy, then leader of The Sons of Calydon, paid off Lighter's debt and took him in, since he was a "high-profile fighter" that rarely lost. At first, Lighter considered the biker gang to be just another boxing show, but over time he became close with all the members and genuinely wanted to protect them and not lose any more friends.

Ju Fufu(橘福福) is a disciple of Yunkui Summit-and occasionally a Hollow Investigator. Fufu is a petite and cheerful demihuman tiger Thiren. She looks small and is still young, but she's the most senior disciple of Suibian Temple and one of Yixuan's first disciples.
"Able to deal with anything," "can even make Shifu listen," "super patient and meticulous teacher," "has great taste when choosing restaurants for teambuilding."
—Yunkui Summit trainee performance review, peer evaluation
"Always messing up the tasks given by Shifu," "doesn't command respect in speech or combat," "doesn't know how to act tough against bad guys," "juniors are more skilled."
—Yunkui Summit trainee performance review, self-evaluation
"'The most skilled senior ever who can do anything, a righteous hero with righteous might' is what I heard her say when I passed by her room. Hm... I concur."
—Yunkui Summit trainee performance review, Shifu's evaluation
Appearance: Green eyes and long hair that is yellow on one side and white on the other, with brown patterns resembling tiger stripes. Tiger-like ears and a long, fluffy striped tail adorned with a navy-blue bow. Her outfit includes a gray/black ensemble with golden accents and an ochre yellow jacket, navy blue shoes, a wooden plaque on her neck, A brown gourd hangs around her hip.
Her weapons are metal claw knuckles. Her W-Engine, Hu We, is a Chibi metal tiger that can move and attack on its own, or serve as an alchemical cauldron or popcorn machine.
Fufu sometimes speaks very loudly (a common trait of tiger Thirens), and then apologizes immediately.
Trivia: Her name may come from mispronunciation of Chinese word "虎". The plaque she wears as a pendant reads "观主值日", which may mean she is temporarily left in charge of Yunkui Summit when master Yi Xuan is away.

Yixuan is the 13th High Preceptor of Yunkui Summit and a Void Hunter-level investigator, officially recognized by Mayor Mayflower, addressed as Shifu (师父) by her disciples. 
Yixuan has golden eyes with piercing gaze, long white hair, large bosom, low-cut black leotard with two groin cutouts, fingerless gloves, yellow jacket rich in Taoist imaginary like trigrams, Tai Chi Diagrams, and coin charms; asymmetrical knee socks and black boots.
She was once an orphan, wandering the world with her sister from a young age. Later, the then-High Preceptor of Yunkui Summit took them in, and they became disciples, learning the craft of Yunkui Summit. During this time, Yixuan demonstrated exceptional talent. No matter the technique, she would quickly master it, not only understanding it thoroughly but also creating her own unique techniques. The Qingming Bird was one of the special techniques she and her sister developed together, which helped her eliminate enemies and banish evil spirits in various difficult and dangerous situations. After becoming the sect's High Preceptor, she accepted an invitation from Mayor Mayflower and joined the Hollow Investigative Association as a partnered special investigator.
After becoming High Preceptor, Yixuan took in many talented disciples to continue Yunkui Summit's lineage. However, her approach is rather casual, and often, even when teaching by example, her disciples can only guess her intentions... Yet, Yixuan isn't troubled by this, as she believes there's no standard answer to the so-called "right path." What matters to her is that her disciples find their own "right path." Of course, if they can understand and learn on their own, that's even better. As their Shifu, she simply lets things unfold naturally.
Yi Xuan can control Auric Ink, which can create black wings behind her, energy explosions, and summon the black ink bird familiar Qingming. She is also good at calligraphy, Feng shui and Qimen Dunjia, etc. Yixuan is pragmatic and not averse to using science or modern methods to solve problems.
When Yixuan was young, she loved eating char siu buns with her sister. but after the fall of the old capital, she lost all interest in them overnight and hasn't eaten them much since. The disciples of Yunkui Summit are unaware of this, and just think their master is a bit picky.

Yunkui Summit(云岿山) is a cultivation sect. Their temple, Suibian Temple is in the Failume Heights. Their members usually wear yellow daopao.
Noteworthy members:
Yixuan (Leader, female human)
Ju Fufu (petite female demihuman tiger Thiren)
Pan Yinhu (male panda Thiren, head chef and financial manager)
Ye Shiyuan (male demihuman Thiren)
The Phaethon siblings would become Yunkui Summit disciples after a series of events.

Phaethon's Melody: A peculiar Drive Disc created by fanatical followers of Phaethon. The eccentric singer repeats an odd melody with a strange voice, striving to spread the legendary deeds of "Lord Phaethon."
It is suggested that it was created by Vivian, and the lyrics describe Phaethon's Bangboo, Eous

Defense Force - Silver Squad was a squad under the Obsidian Division of the Defense Force that got wiped out during the Fall of the Old Capital, with Anby (the squad leader) and Soldier 11 being the only survivors.
The main purpose of this squad is to mass produce replica soldiers. However, due to unknown reasons, other replicas cannot stably inherit the combat capability of No. 0 (Anby), and this project was shelved.
Features of Silver Squad units:
-Petite girls with white hair, amber eyes, wearing black and white dresses, black kneesocks. Although they look the same, each has a different personality.
-Relatively resilient body. Immune to anesthetics. But they do feel pain.
-After seriously injured, they will enter a recovery state, usually lasts for a few days. They lose memories 24 hours after that injury (maybe a measure to prevent PTSD).
Since most mass-produced units do not have sufficient combat capabilities, they are mostly used in suicide missions.
Due to budget cuts, Silver Squad decided to keep only one subject. Anby slit her own neck when she was ordered to kill No. 11, forcing researchers to keep No. 11. Her lead researcher, out of sympathy, anonymously contacted Nicole to pick her up, though Anby does not remember any details due to the aforementioned memory-related issue. Afterwards, No. 11 was assigned to the Obsidian Division's OBOL Squad as Soldier 11. Unknown to Anby and No. 11, another replica individual named Twiggy also survived and was secretly planning to revive the Silver Squad.

Twiggy was one of the members of the Silver Squad. She was the main antagonist of Soldier 0 - Anby's Agent Story: Echoes of Silver. She has the same appearance as other Silver Squad units; except she wears a black eye patch on her left eye and has prosthetic limbs.
Cunning and smart, during her days in the Silver Squad, Twiggy made up for her lack of combat prowess with strategy and backup planning. The tortures she endured after being found limbless in the rubble made her cynical and heartless, although deep down she nurtured a strong desire to go back when her "family" was alive, together and "happy", hence the attempt at reforming the Silver Company.
History
Twiggy was one of the replicas used as soldiers in the Defense Force Obsidian Division Silver Squad. Like most younger "sisters" of Anby, she was not as well-versed in combat as her captain, and made up for it with her intelligence and strategy, coming up with battle formations and backup plans.
When the Silver Squad was sent on a suicide mission, Twiggy survived under the rubble and the corpses of her fellow squamates, but all her limbs got severely corrupted, leading to amputation. The Hollow Raiders who found her sold her off on the black market, where she got treated like merchandise and traded multiple times, until she was bought by an ex military doctor gone rogue, who gave her prosthetic limbs and the chance to form the Silver Squad once again: combining incomplete research data stolen from the Defense Force archives with her memories, Twiggy managed to revive the technology behind replica creation. Nonetheless, the data in Twiggy's hands was not enough to create replicas with fighting abilities, but only "defects" like Girl A, so she used her newfound "sisters" for organ harvesting to fund her research and get in touch with TOPS. Her allies also managed to kidnap Soldier 11. Discovered by Anby and Trigger, Twiggy tried to persuade the ex-captain to join her in the reformation of the Silver Squad; however, Anby was of the opposite idea, deeming the Silver Company as evil technology that should never resurface again.
When faced with Anby's refusal and the guilt of what she did to her fellow replicas, she began injecting herself with a serum provided by her client, only to get Girl A to snatch the syringe from her hands and inject herself with the other half. Under the effect of the serum, the two turned into the Ethereal Palicus, and were defeated by Soldier 0 - Anby and Soldier 11. In her dying moments, the corrupted Twiggy was still able to talk, briefly reliving her memories as a member of the Silver Squad under the care of Anby before passing away.

Girl A was one of the replicas created by Twiggy in Soldier 0 - Anby's Agent Story: Echoes of Silver. She has the same appearance as other Silver Squad units except she wears a black mask.
Profile
Although deemed by Twiggy as an idiot and a defect unable to talk, Girl A was an intelligent and compassionate being, grateful to Twiggy for creating her and protective towards her fellow replicas.
History
Girl A was one of the replicas Twiggy created during her attempt at recreating the Silver Squad, likely the first successful clone, given her name. She assisted Twiggy in carrying out her plan, taking care of her wounds and warning her when Anby infiltrated the laboratory. She tried to make Twiggy desist from bypassing the middleman to contact the client directly, to no avail.
When facing Anby's refusal to help revive the Silver Squad, Twiggy began injecting herself with the serum provided by the client, but Girl A snatched the syringe from her hands and injected herself with the other half in a desperate attempt to protect Twiggy and the other replicas. While Twiggy tried to scold her for her rash action, Girl A replied, talking for the first time: she expressed her gratitude to Twiggy for creating her, and the desire to never let her be lonely. Under the effect of the serum, the two turned into the Ethereal Palicus, and were defeated by Soldier 0 - Anby and Soldier 11.

Palicus is an Ether Mutant encountered during combat. They are transformed from Girl A and Twiggy respectively. Ethereals that are always seen in pairs, distinguished by their peculiar physical traits. A profound bond exists between the two.
"Whoa, that was close! Don't just deal with one. You gotta watch out for the ladies' sneak attacks!"
"Hold on, 'ladies'? Has being in the Hollow for so long made you see even the Ethereals as attractive?"
"Dude, I know Ethereals and I know ladies. I swear, Palicus are definitely 'ladies'."
"No way!"
"That long hair? Those legs? They're like total 'factory-made babes' — my sister taught me that term."
"How's your sister doing lately?"
"She's actually getting better. But next month's medical bills? Gotta hustle that cash from these Ethereal hotties."
"Ain't easy being an older brother, eh? Here's the deal: I'll keep Palicus busy, and you give me your sister's number."
"Hah. Like I'm actually gonna give you her number!"
—The last words of two young Hollow Raiders
Combat Info
In combat, the Palicus fight by combining both close and ranged attacks. When one dies, the other will enter a Frenzy state, increasing the speed and ferocity of its attacks.
